emergency_instructions.title: Severe Toothache (Including After 8:00 PM)
emergency_instructions.description: If you have a severe, throbbing toothache that does not improve with over‑the‑counter pain relievers, you may have an infection or abscess that requires urgent care. Do not ignore worsening pain, fever, facial swelling, or difficulty swallowing.
emergency_instructions.self_care_steps: Rinse gently with warm salt water, floss carefully around the painful tooth to remove trapped food, and take an over‑the‑counter pain reliever as directed if you are able. Do NOT place aspirin directly on the tooth or gums, and avoid very hot or cold foods and drinks.
emergency_instructions.notes: For severe toothache occurring after 8:00 PM (for example, around 9:00 PM), call the after‑hours emergency number and leave a detailed voicemail with your name, phone number, symptoms, and when the pain began. If you develop trouble breathing or swallowing, call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room.

emergency_instructions.title: Broken or Chipped Tooth
emergency_instructions.description: A broken, cracked, or chipped tooth can be painful and may expose the inner layers of the tooth, increasing the risk of infection.
emergency_instructions.self_care_steps: Rinse your mouth gently with warm water. If there is bleeding, apply gentle pressure with clean gauze. Save any broken pieces in milk or saline if possible. Avoid chewing on the affected side.
emergency_instructions.notes: Call immediately if you have significant pain, a large portion of the tooth is missing, or there is sharp edge cutting your tongue or cheek. Minor chips without pain can typically be seen within a few days during normal business hours.

emergency_instructions.title: Knocked‑Out Permanent Tooth
emergency_instructions.description: A knocked‑out permanent tooth is a true dental emergency. Prompt treatment (ideally within 30–60 minutes) gives the best chance to save the tooth.
emergency_instructions.self_care_steps: Handle the tooth only by the crown (chewing surface), not the root. If dirty, gently rinse with milk or saline without scrubbing. If possible, place the tooth back into the socket and hold it in place by biting gently on gauze. If you cannot reinsert it, keep the tooth moist in milk or a tooth preservation kit.
emergency_instructions.notes: Do not let the tooth dry out. Do not scrub or remove any attached tissue fragments. Seek emergency dental care immediately. For children, primary (baby) teeth that are knocked out are generally NOT reinserted—call for guidance.

emergency_instructions.title: Uncontrolled Oral Bleeding
emergency_instructions.description: Bleeding after dental work or an injury should gradually slow down with firm pressure. If bleeding is heavy or does not decrease after 20–30 minutes, it requires urgent assessment.
emergency_instructions.self_care_steps: Place a clean, folded piece of gauze or a damp tea bag over the area and bite down with firm, continuous pressure for 20 minutes without checking. Sit upright and avoid spitting or using straws.
emergency_instructions.notes: If bleeding is profuse, you feel light‑headed, or you cannot control it with pressure, call the after‑hours number immediately. If you are on blood thinners or have a bleeding disorder and cannot reach us quickly, call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room.

emergency_instructions.title: Swelling or Suspected Dental Infection
emergency_instructions.description: Facial swelling, warmth, or redness near a tooth or jaw, especially with fever or feeling ill, can indicate a spreading dental infection that should not be ignored.
emergency_instructions.self_care_steps: Keep your head elevated, apply a cold compress to the outside of your cheek in 10‑minute intervals, and take over‑the‑counter pain medicine if you can. Do not apply heat to the face and do not attempt to drain any swelling yourself.
emergency_instructions.notes: Call our office or after‑hours emergency line right away if the swelling is increasing, you have trouble swallowing, or you notice difficulty breathing—these can be signs of a serious infection requiring hospital care.

insurance_plans.name: Delta Dental PPO
insurance_plans.notes: Typical coverage: 100% preventive (cleanings, exams, routine X‑rays), 80% basic, 50% major after deductible. Orthodontic benefits often limited to children up to age 19; check your specific policy.

insurance_plans.name: Cigna Dental
insurance_plans.notes: Most Cigna PPO plans we see cover preventive services at 80% in‑network after any applicable waiting periods. Some plans may offer enhanced preventive benefits for certain conditions.

insurance_plans.name: MetLife Dental PPO
insurance_plans.notes: Commonly covers two cleanings per year at 90% in‑network. Orthodontic coverage may apply to dependent children; confirm your group plan details.

insurance_plans.name: Aetna DMO
insurance_plans.notes: Members must select our office as their primary care dentist through Aetna. Copays vary by procedure; preventive services are typically covered at 100% with low or no copay.

locations.name: Downtown Clinic
locations.notes: Primary location for online bookings, including weekday evening appointments starting at 5:00 PM or later.

locations.name: Northside Family Dental
locations.notes: Offers pediatric dentistry with Saturday appointments, including late‑morning and early‑afternoon times.

locations.name: Lakeside Dental Center
locations.notes: Smaller location focusing on adult preventive and restorative care during standard daytime hours.

services.name: Adult Teeth Cleaning
services.description: Routine preventive cleaning for adults, including removal of plaque and tartar above the gumline and polishing of teeth.
services.price_notes: Includes standard cleaning and polishing; does not include X‑rays or deep cleaning.

services.name: Adult Preventive Cleaning (Routine)
services.description: Routine 6‑month preventive cleaning for adults, often covered at a high percentage by PPO insurance plans.
services.price_notes: Billed as a periodic preventive visit; exam and routine X‑rays may be billed separately depending on insurance.

services.name: Adult New Patient Exam & Cleaning
services.description: Comprehensive new patient visit for adults, including full exam, necessary X‑rays, and standard cleaning if appropriate.
services.price_notes: Price may vary if deep cleaning or additional imaging is required.

services.name: Adult Comprehensive Oral Exam
services.description: Detailed oral exam for adults, including gum health assessment, oral cancer screening, and treatment planning.
services.price_notes: Often scheduled with a separate cleaning appointment.

services.name: Child New Patient Exam (Under 13)
services.description: First visit for new pediatric patients under age 13, including exam, age‑appropriate X‑rays, and cleaning if recommended.
services.price_notes: Designed for children ages 3–12; includes parent consultation and home care instructions.

services.name: Pediatric Exam & Cleaning
services.description: Routine exam and cleaning visit for established pediatric patients, recommended every 6 months.
services.price_notes: Fluoride treatment and sealants, if needed, may have additional charges.

services.name: Root Canal (Molar)
services.description: Endodontic treatment to remove infected or inflamed pulp tissue from a molar tooth, followed by sealing of the root canals.
services.price_notes: Does not include the cost of the final crown restoration; fees may vary with tooth complexity.

services.name: Porcelain Crown (Molar)
services.description: Tooth‑colored porcelain crown for a molar tooth, designed to restore strength and appearance after root canal or fracture.
services.price_notes: Includes custom lab‑fabricated porcelain crown; price assumes standard materials and shade selection.

services.name: Ceramic Crown (Molar)
services.description: All‑ceramic crown option for molars, offering a natural appearance with high strength.
services.price_notes: Price may vary depending on specific ceramic material selected.

services.name: Full Metal Crown (Molar)
services.description: Durable full‑metal crown for molar teeth, typically used on back teeth where esthetics are less critical.
services.price_notes: Often the most cost‑effective crown option for molars; alloy selection may affect final fee.

services.name: Invisalign Consultation
services.description: One‑on‑one consultation to determine whether Invisalign clear aligners are a good option for straightening your teeth.
services.price_notes: Consultation fee may be applied toward treatment if you begin Invisalign within 60 days.

services.name: Invisalign Full Treatment
services.description: Comprehensive Invisalign treatment using nearly invisible clear aligners to straighten teeth, typically completed in 12–18 months for most adult cases.
services.price_notes: Total fee depends on case complexity; flexible payment plans are available.

services.name: Traditional Metal Braces (Comprehensive)
services.description: Comprehensive orthodontic treatment with traditional metal braces, suitable for children, teens, and adults.
services.price_notes: Typical treatment time ranges from 18–24 months depending on case complexity.

services.name: Whitening Consultation
services.description: Consultation to evaluate stains and discuss in‑office and take‑home teeth whitening options.
services.price_notes: New‑patient whitening promotions may reduce or waive this fee when scheduled with a qualifying offer.

services.name: In‑Office Teeth Whitening
services.description: Professional in‑office whitening treatment to brighten teeth multiple shades in a single visit.
services.price_notes: Promotional pricing may be available for new patients; sensitivity products may be recommended after treatment.

services.name: Emergency Exam / Limited Problem Visit
services.description: Urgent visit focused on a specific problem such as severe toothache, swelling, or broken tooth, with limited X‑rays as needed.
services.price_notes: Does not include the cost of definitive treatment (such as root canal or extraction).

services.name: Virtual Dental Consultation
services.description: Video consultation to review concerns, second opinions, or treatment options without an in‑office visit.
services.price_notes: Not suitable for all emergencies; in‑person evaluation may still be required.

dentists.biography: Dr. Emma Cho is a general dentist with a special interest in preventive and cosmetic dentistry. She enjoys helping anxious patients feel at ease and focuses on conservative, evidence-based care. Dr. Cho offers extended weekday evening hours at our Downtown Clinic to accommodate busy professionals.

Dr. Liam Patel provides comprehensive general dentistry with an emphasis on restorative care, including molar root canals and crowns. He collaborates closely with our endodontic and prosthodontic team members to restore function and comfort.

Dr. Olivia Nguyen is a board-certified pediatric dentist who loves helping children build positive, lifelong habits at the dentist. She offers gentle care for infants, children, and pre-teens, and provides Saturday appointments at our Northside Family Dental location.

Dr. Noah Ross is an orthodontist offering Invisalign clear aligners as well as traditional metal and ceramic braces. He enjoys helping adult and teen patients choose the option that best matches their lifestyle and timeline.

Dr. Ava Martinez focuses on cosmetic and restorative dentistry, including professional whitening and smile makeovers. She primarily practices at Lakeside Dental Center and welcomes new adult patients seeking to improve their smile aesthetics.

Dr. William Hughes is an endodontist with advanced training in root canal therapy and the management of dental infections. He provides same- and next-day appointments for severe tooth pain at the Downtown Clinic.
Dr. Sophia Khan is a family dentist at Northside Family Dental who provides comprehensive care for adults and older children. She offers limited Saturday hours for routine cleanings and exams.

new_patient_guides.title: New Adult Patients: What to Expect at Your First Visit
new_patient_guides.description: Most new adult patients begin with a comprehensive exam and cleaning so we can evaluate your teeth, gums, and overall oral health and create a personalized treatment plan.
new_patient_guides.additional_instructions: Please arrive 10 minutes early to complete paperwork, bring a list of medications, and have your dental insurance card ready if applicable. If you have recent X-rays (within the last 12 months), you may request to have them forwarded to our office in advance.

new_patient_guides.title: New Child Patients (Under 13): First Visit Guide
new_patient_guides.description: For children under 13, we recommend a dedicated child new patient exam that focuses on comfort, growth and development, and preventive care education for both kids and parents.
new_patient_guides.additional_instructions: A parent or legal guardian must attend the entire visit and remain in the office. For young or anxious children, you may bring a favorite toy or comfort item. Please inform us in advance of any special needs or behavioral considerations so we can prepare appropriately.

orthodontic_treatment_options.name: Invisalign Clear Aligners for Adults
orthodontic_treatment_options.description: Invisalign uses a series of clear, removable aligners to gradually straighten teeth. The smooth plastic trays are nearly invisible and can be removed for eating, brushing, and special occasions. Most adult treatments are completed in 1218 months, with complex cases occasionally approaching 24 months.

orthodontic_treatment_options.name: Traditional Metal Braces
orthodontic_treatment_options.description: Traditional metal braces use brackets and wires to move teeth into alignment. They are visible on the teeth but highly effective for mild to complex orthodontic needs. Typical full treatments range from 18 to 24 months.

orthodontic_treatment_options.name: Ceramic (Tooth-Colored) Braces
orthodontic_treatment_options.description: Ceramic braces use tooth-colored or clear brackets that blend in more with the teeth, making them less noticeable than traditional metal braces. Treatment time is similar to metal braces, often between 18 and 24 months.

orthodontic_treatment_options.name: Express Clear Aligner Treatment
orthodontic_treatment_options.description: Shorter clear aligner programs are available for mild spacing or crowding concerns. These are nearly invisible like Invisalign but designed for limited tooth movement, usually completed in 679 months.

promotions.name: New Patient In-Office Whitening Special - $199
promotions.description: Limited-time cosmetic whitening offer for new adult patients. Includes an in-office whitening session at a special new patient price.
promotions.eligibility_notes: Valid for new adult patients only. Cannot be combined with other whitening discounts. Standard exam may be required prior to whitening to confirm eligibility.
promotions.terms_and_conditions: Offer valid only at participating locations. Patient must be at least 18 years old and clinically eligible for whitening. Additional visits or touch-up kits not included.

promotions.name: New Patient Exam & Cleaning for $99
promotions.description: Comprehensive new patient exam and routine cleaning for adults at a special introductory price.
promotions.eligibility_notes: For new adult patients without significant periodontal disease. If deep cleaning is required, promotional value may be applied toward alternative treatment.
promotions.terms_and_conditions: Insurance cannot be billed in conjunction with this offer. Includes standard X-rays as determined by the dentist.

promotions.name: Free Whitening with Invisalign Treatment
promotions.description: Start full Invisalign treatment and receive complimentary in-office whitening at the end of your case.
promotions.eligibility_notes: Available for new or existing patients who start comprehensive Invisalign treatment during the promotional period.
promotions.terms_and_conditions: Whitening is provided after active Invisalign treatment is complete and teeth are free of active decay or gum disease.

promotions.name: Holiday Whitening Special - $149
promotions.description: Seasonal in-office whitening promotion that has ended. Shown for reference only.
promotions.eligibility_notes: Offer expired and no longer available to redeem.
promotions.terms_and_conditions: Previously limited to one use per patient; cannot be reactivated.

Weekday afternoon child new patient exam, ideal for a 10-year-old.
Additional weekday afternoon child new patient exam slot.
Afternoon Invisalign consultation for adults comparing clear aligners versus braces.
Morning Invisalign consultation slot suitable for adults seeking nearly invisible treatment.
Saturday late-morning Invisalign consultation at Northside location.
Morning whitening consultation within the current month's $199 whitening promotion period.
Another current-month morning whitening consultation option for new patients.
In-office whitening treatment slot that can be booked after a consultation and promotion claim.
Same-day emergency exam slot for severe toothache or infection at Downtown Clinic.
Routine adult preventive cleaning useful for booking with higher-coverage insurance plans.
Afternoon adult preventive cleaning time for insured or uninsured patients.
